text.title: Signal Sources for Trend Analysis
text.summary: Technology, market, and consumer signal sources with known biases and collection tools for each.

list.item: Hacker News (Algolia API): developer sentiment and new-tool discussion — free, no key, 30-day window
Product Hunt (GraphQL API): new products and category emergence — free tier available
GitHub (REST API): star velocity as adoption proxy — filter by created date, sort by stars
arXiv (pip install arxiv): paper counts as research-momentum signal — category + date filter
VC blogs (RSS): investment thesis patterns — lagging but high-signal (a16z Big Ideas, etc.)

rule.statement: Use GitHub star velocity (new repos in last 180 days + top star count) as an early technology-trend signal, not absolute star count.
rule.rationale: Absolute star count rewards incumbents. Star velocity captures acceleration — the signal that matters for timing entry.

list.item: Google Trends (pytrends): search interest over time — smoothed on low-volume terms, splits on term renames
Exploding Topics: curated emerging topics with growth rate — good seed source, Pro plan has API
Crunchbase / PitchBook: funding velocity — paid API, hard gate if key missing (fail loudly)
LinkedIn Jobs / Indeed: skill keyword volume — hiring volume is strongest lagging-validation signal
Gartner Hype Cycle / Forrester: authoritative for B2B but lag inflection by 12-18 months

rule.statement: Score deltas, not absolutes: last-month vs this-month change in HN hits / star velocity beats raw counts.
rule.rationale: Absolute counts reward historical incumbents. Month-over-month deltas surface accelerating momentum regardless of baseline.

list.item: Reddit (PRAW): subreddit subscriber growth and post velocity — free API, strong for validating intensity
TikTok Creative Center: emerging consumer behaviors — limited API, scrape with care
Amazon bestsellers: product category shifts — no API, manual or Playwright scrape
App Store rankings: category leader emergence — Apptopia/Sensor Tower API for trend data

antipattern.description: Letting the agent pick its own sources for numeric evidence.
antipattern.reason: Unconstrained source selection collapses to "search Google, cite first SEO-spam page". Pin the allow-list of authoritative sources in the system prompt.

antipattern.description: Using Google Trends on a low-volume term without checking if the term was recently renamed.
antipattern.reason: "AI agent" and "agentic AI" split the trend curve. Synonym-merge before scoring to avoid treating the same trend as two different signals.

antipattern.description: Treating a single viral HN or Reddit thread as a trend confirmation.
antipattern.reason: Require >= 3 independent sources before stage-promoting a trend. One viral post is a data point, not a trend.
